name: Brooks, Meg
evidence: Could not corroborate identity. The only public Austin 'Meg Brooks' with a professional footprint is an attorney (State Bar #03070775, licensed 1990, criminal law and wills/trusts/probate, formerly Travis County District Attorney's Office, license now Inactive), but her listed practice address is Austin 78720 (a PO-box ZIP), not the donor's residential 78723-1932, so the match is name-only and not corroborated by ZIP, occupation (filed blank), or donation pattern. A separate name-only hit on mineralholders.com for a 'Meg Brooks' of Austin holding 5 mineral interests had no ZIP corroboration and was deliberately NOT recorded as an oil_gas affiliation. The 159-donation / 0.957 Democratic-lean FEC profile is consistent with a small-dollar recurring ActBlue donor but returned no name+ZIP-matched contributor record disclosing an employer.

name: Hart, Kevin
evidence: Not identifiable. 'Kevin Hart' is an extremely common name further swamped in search by the comedian; no Cedar Park / 78613 resident by that name could be located in LinkedIn, business, news, obituary, or Cedar Park civic/candidate sources. The nearest Cedar Park matches are different people (Brian Hart, Southwest Airlines pilot, 78613; Kevin Harris, Cedar Park City Council member). The 261-donation FEC record at a near-neutral 0.599 lean is almost certainly a name-collision aggregate across multiple unrelated Kevin Harts nationally rather than one Cedar Park donor, and so is not usable as identity corroboration. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration for Kevin Hart in any registry year searched. Filed occupation blank, no employer string to fall back on, so no industry assigned.

name: Bush, Allison
resolved_employer: Public education advocate and fundraiser; former Executive Director, Pflugerville ISD Education Foundation; 2026 Democratic candidate for Texas State Board of Education District 5
evidence: Confirmed by exact ZIP match: Teach the Vote (ATPE) lists candidate Allison Bush as Democrat, Pflugerville, TX 78660, occupation 'Business Development - Education', matching the donor's Pflugerville 78660-6375 address; her Democratic-primary candidacy and 1.0 Democratic FEC lean over 6 donations are consistent with the 2025-08-31 gift to Travis County Commissioner Jeff Travillion. Full bio read: 25+ years in education as teacher, school counselor, and nonprofit executive — Executive Director of the Pflugerville Education Foundation, Partnerships Coordinator for Bastrop ISD, career/college readiness counselor at American YouthWorks, theater and speech teacher at Crosby ISD.

affiliations.org: Allison Bush for Texas State Board of Education, District 5

affiliations.snippet: Listed as Democrat, Pflugerville TX 78660, candidate for Texas State Board of Education District 5; advanced to a runoff in the 2026 Democratic primary, endorsed by the Austin Chronicle and multiple school board members.

Bio lists her as Executive Director of the Pflugerville ISD/Pflugerville Education Foundation, where she created a grant reform system that raised teacher grant applications from 32 to 231 and generated roughly $500,000 in funding.

Listed among her board memberships and volunteer roles alongside two ISD education foundations and the Texas Association of Partners in Education.

name: Yvonne M. Williams Campaign, J.P.
resolved_employer: Campaign committee of Judge Yvonne M. Williams, Travis County Justice of the Peace, Precinct 1 (not an individual donor)
evidence: Confirmed as a political committee, not a person: this is the campaign of Judge Yvonne Michelle Williams, elected Travis County Justice of the Peace Precinct 1 in November 2010, seated January 2011, term running through December 31, 2026. The committee's Texas Ethics Commission mailing address is PO Box 142248, Austin, TX 78714-2248 (treasurer Albert Black), which matches the filed donor ZIP of 78714 and confirms the entity; the 2017-10-23 gift to Commissioner Brigid Shea is a committee-to-committee transfer between two Travis County Democratic officeholders. Full bio read: Williams previously served as an Associate Municipal Court Judge for the City of Austin and as a hearings officer for the then Texas Workers' Compensation Commission, and began as a civil rights trial attorney in federal court in the 1980s. Because the filer is a committee rather than a natural person, the FEC individual-contributor and lobbyist-registry checks returned nothing attributable.

affiliations.org: Yvonne M. Williams Campaign (Justice of the Peace, Precinct 1, Travis County)
affiliations.snippet: Yvonne Williams is a judge for Precinct 1 of the Travis County Justice of the Peace in Texas; she assumed office in 2011 and her current term ends December 31, 2026. She won the Democratic primary in 2018 and again in 2022.

affiliations.org: Travis County Justice of the Peace, Precinct 1
affiliations.snippet: Travis County's official Precinct 1 page identifies Judge Yvonne M. Williams as the sitting Justice of the Peace for Precinct 1.

name: Bedrosian, Arthur
resolved_employer: International Veterinary Sciences LLC (CEO & partner since June 2018); formerly President/CEO of Lannett Company Inc., generic pharmaceuticals
evidence: Arthur P. Bedrosian, longtime CEO of generic-drug maker Lannett Company (President 2002, CEO 2006-2018), became CEO/partner of International Veterinary Sciences in June 2018; his LinkedIn lists him in the Austin, Texas Metropolitan Area, consistent with the 78735 (southwest Austin) address and with a donation to Commissioner Ann Howard, whose precinct covers that area. Surname is rare and no other Arthur Bedrosian is associated with Austin, but no public record directly ties him to the 78735 address, so identity rests on name+metro rather than a documented address. Note the tension in the record: his only confirmed federal giving is Republican (two 2015 contributions to Sen. Pat Toomey) from his Philadelphia 19107 address during his Lannett tenure, while this local gift went to a Democrat.

evidence: Sharon Zeugin is a working calligrapher, fine artist and graphic-recording practitioner whose sole-proprietor business, Sharon Zeugin Calligraphy, is listed in Austin, TX 78704 — an exact match for the donor's zip on a rare surname. She teaches at the Austin Contemporary Art School, has led the Literally Letters program at Ghost Ranch, NM, trained in calligraphy at the Roehampton Institute (University of Surrey, London), and has a prior background as a therapist/social worker. name: Wallace, Larry
resolved_employer: Dr. Larry Wallace Jr. - President, Wallace Brothers & Associates; U.S. Army officer (retired June 2017); later Mayor of Manor, TX and Chief of Staff to Austin Council Member Natasha Harper-Madison
evidence: Best match is Dr. Larry Wallace Jr., a retired U.S. Army captain (1999-2017, Deputy Chief of Staff, U.S. Army Special Operations Aviation Command; Bronze Star) who is a fixture of East Austin/Travis County Precinct 1 civic life: a Precinct 1 appointee to the Travis County Citizens Bond Advisory Committee, later Chief of Staff to Council Member Natasha Harper-Madison (District 1), and the first African American mayor of Manor, TX in 2019. Travillion is the Precinct 1 commissioner, so the donation pattern corroborates this person over the alternative Austin 'Larry P. Wallace' in aerospace/energy. Confidence held at medium because the May 2017 gift date precedes his Army retirement, no source confirms a 78702 address, and the 0.248 fec_lean across 40 donations conflicts with his documented Democratic affiliation (likely national name-collision noise; he ran as a Democrat for TX-10 in 2022).

name: Carlos Lopez Campaign
resolved_employer: Campaign committee of Carlos B. Lopez, Travis County Constable, Precinct 5 (not an individual donor)
evidence: This is a political committee, not an individual. The only Travis County officeholder named Carlos Lopez is Carlos B. Lopez, elected Constable of Precinct 5 (in office since 2012-2013), a Travis County employee since 1980 who rose from Deputy Constable to Chief Deputy to Constable. He was in office and maintaining an active campaign committee in May 2017, and a county constable's committee contributing to a sitting county commissioner (Travillion) is a routine intra-county officeholder transfer. He is a Democrat, consistent with the fec_lean of 1.0, and 78703 falls within the central-Austin area his precinct covers. Note: 'Justice Precinct 5' in his ballot title refers to the precinct's jurisdiction, not a Justice of the Peace office. Confidence is medium because the committee's own filed address was not independently verified against 78703.

affiliations.role: Elected Constable (since 2012); Travis County law enforcement employee since 1980
affiliations.snippet: Carlos B. Lopez has served in progressively responsible positions within Travis County since 1980, advancing from Deputy Constable to Chief Deputy before assuming the constable position; holds a Master Peace Officer License and received a 45-year Travis County longevity certificate in 2023.

name: Malcolm, Edward
evidence: Could not identify this person. No Austin-area professional profile, bio page, news mention, or public-records listing for an Edward/Ed Malcolm in 78701 (downtown Austin) was found; the only 'Edward Malcolm' profiles located are in the UK (London) and Washington DC, neither corroborated by zip, occupation, or donation pattern. Campaign record lists no occupation. FEC individual-contributor corroboration was attempted but the FEC API returned HTTP 429 and OpenSecrets returned 403 for the duration of the session, so the 29 FEC donations could not be inspected directly. Confirmed NOT a registered lobbyist: absent from both the Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists and the City of Austin City Clerk lobbyist registry. Note the $70 gift to Travillion on 2017-05-10 is the same amount and date as donor Freddie Brown, consistent with a shared fundraiser event.

name: Ciaccia, Ailing

evidence: Rare name resolves to a single person. Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation records show Ailing Ciaccia, Cosmetology Operator, at 5712 Wellington Dr, Austin, TX 78723-3235 — an exact match to the donor's 78723 zip — with a separate 'Salon Ciaccia' cosmetology salon license at the same address, corroborating the reported occupation 'Hairstylist' and employer 'Self'. This is a categorize-by-employer fallback outcome: a self-employed service-business owner with no public policy or influence footprint. All three mandatory searches run and came back clean — no FEC PAC or ideological-committee ties surfaced (her 81 FEC donations are consistent with small-dollar recurring giving; direct FEC API inspection was blocked by HTTP 429), no TEC lobbyist registration, and no City of Austin lobbyist registration. LinkedIn returned HTTP 999 to automated retrieval; the TDLR licensing record was read in full instead and disclosed no board seats or organizational roles.

name: Gregory, Paul
evidence: Common name, not resolvable to a single person — deliberately left unclassified rather than guessed. Two Austin candidates exist and neither can be tied to the donor: (1) a 'Paul L Gregory' listed in people-search aggregators at an Austin 78704 address, matching the donor zip but carrying no occupation or employer information; and (2) a Paul Gregory who is Director of Recycling and Organics at Texas Disposal Systems in Austin, whose employer would be a notable finding given TDS's landfill-permit and municipal-contract interests and given that recipient Brigid Shea is the county commissioner most associated with environmental policy — but no residence zip or address corroborates him, and the TDS org-chart page was fetched and read in full and does not list any Paul Gregory. Treating the TDS role as this donor's employer would be an unsupported inference, so no industry is assigned. No FEC record. Confirmed NOT a registered lobbyist in either the TEC or City of Austin registries.

name: Renteria, SolAna
resolved_employer: Self-employed - owner/operator, Salon 1936 (hair salon, Central East Austin)
evidence: SolAna Renteria is a born-and-raised East Austinite who owns and operates Salon 1936, a full-service hair salon at 1511 Haskell St in the East Cesar Chavez neighborhood, run out of her childhood home (a historic 1936 bungalow). She began beauty school in 2002 and trained at Bumble and Bumble University (NY), Wella Color and Pivot Point. The distinctive spelling 'SolAna' plus the filing's occupation 'Hairstylist' / employer 'Self' in Austin corroborates identity to a near-unique degree; the 78744 filing zip is residential and differs from the salon's 78702 business zip. IMPORTANT: no public source connects her to former Austin City Council D3 member Sabino 'Pio' Renteria or to Susana Almanza — the shared surname and East Austin geography are suggestive but UNSOURCED, and no family link is asserted. Full read of her Salon 1936 About page shows no board seats, civic offices, or policy-advocacy roles. TEC lobbyist search returned no registration. The 4 FEC donations could not be individually itemized (FEC API HTTP 429), so no federal PAC affiliation is claimed.

affiliations.snippet: Salon 1936 is 'a modern, full service hair salon located in my childhood home, a historic 1936 bungalow in Central East Austin,' owned and operated by SolAna Renteria, a 'born and raised East Austinite.'

name: Jackson, Edward
resolved_employer: Travis County Sheriff's Office - Captain, Corrections Bureau (Inmate Logistics and Processing)
evidence: Matches Captain Edwardo Jackson on the official TCSO command-staff roster: born in Austin, joined TCSO in 2000 as a Security Coordinator, became a Corrections Officer in 2004, and was appointed Captain by Sheriff Sally Hernandez in April 2022, overseeing the Central Booking Facility, Travis County Jail, Records and Property. He holds Certified Jail Manager, Master Jailer and Peace Officer licenses and is a National Jail Leadership Command Academy (Class #43) graduate. The filing's self-reported occupation 'TCSO' / employer 'TCSO Captain' matches the roster exactly, and he is the only Jackson at captain rank in the agency. NAME CAVEAT: the filing reads 'Edward' while the official roster and his LinkedIn read 'Edwardo' — treated as a filing-entry truncation, not a different person, given the unique rank+agency match. Driftwood/78619 is a Hays County suburb within commuting range of TCSO facilities; residence zip is not independently confirmed. No FEC contribution record. No TEC lobbyist registration.

affiliations.role: President (2024 term); Board member and Awards Committee co-chair; member since 2015
affiliations.snippet: TJA's official past-presidents list records Edwardo Jackson as president for 2024, with a traviscountytx.gov contact address. TCSO's command-staff bio adds that he has been an active TJA member since 2015, served the 2023-2024 presidential term, and received TJA Distinguished Service Awards in 2019 and 2022.
